thesis: Texas Pacific Land Corporation owns roughly 882,000 surface acres and royalty interests across the Permian Basin. **[Fact]** FY2025 consolidated revenue was $798.2M; oil and gas royalties $411.7M; water services $307.5M. **[Fact]** GAAP book (~$21/sh) excludes 1888 Assigned land and royalties carried at zero. **[Inference]** TPL is a scarce-asset toll collector on Permian drilling, surface access, and water infrastructure rather than a traditional operator.
why_market_wrong: The market may compare price to GAAP book (~19×) while core acreage is off balance sheet at historical zero. **[Inference]** Lawrence owner-cash IRR answers current yield at price; a separate component schedule values risked royalty inventory, surface acreage, and milestone options. Proof-first base components sum to ~$246/sh versus ~$414 price, suggesting the market embeds Permian scarcity and optionality beyond filing-locked operating cash paths.

key_assumptions.source: FY2025 company-defined FCF less SBC / 69.027M diluted shares (`10-K_20260218`)

open_questions: Tract-level NRA roll-forward separating producing, visible, and dormant acreage.
Data-center, desalination, and corridor milestone capital waterfalls and contracted economics.
Governance and capital allocation post Horizon Kinetics transition ([HUMAN REVIEW]).
